# Concrete Knowhow — Full Reference

All calculations are performed client-side. Results are estimates — always verify with your supplier and add 5–10% overage for real projects.

---

## Concrete Slab Calculator

URL: https://concrete.mrknowitall.net/concrete/

### Formulas
- Rectangular slab: vol_ft³ = length_ft × width_ft × thickness_ft
- Round slab: vol_ft³ = π × (diameter_ft / 2)² × thickness_ft
- vol_yd³ = vol_ft³ ÷ 27
- 60 lb bags = ceil(vol_ft³ ÷ 0.45)
- 80 lb bags = ceil(vol_ft³ ÷ 0.60)
- Ready-mix is more economical above ~1 cubic yard (≈ 45 bags of 80 lb)

### Common Questions
Q: How many bags of concrete for a 10×10 slab at 4 inches thick?
A: 33.3 cu ft = 1.23 yd³. You need 75 bags of 60 lb mix or 56 bags of 80 lb mix.

Q: How thick should a concrete slab be?
A: Patios and driveways: 4 inches. Garage floors: 4–6 inches. Heavy-load slabs: 6–8 inches.

Q: When does ready-mix make more sense than bags?
A: Above roughly 1 cubic yard. At that volume the labor of mixing bags costs more than delivery.

Q: How much does a cubic yard of concrete weigh?
A: Approximately 3,900–4,050 lbs (about 2 tons) when wet.

Q: Should I add overage when ordering concrete?
A: Yes — add 5–10%. Subgrades are rarely perfectly level and running short mid-pour is a major problem.

---

## Gravel Calculator

URL: https://concrete.mrknowitall.net/gravel/

### Formulas
- vol_ft³ = length_ft × width_ft × depth_ft
- vol_yd³ = vol_ft³ ÷ 27
- tons = vol_yd³ × bulk_density

### Bulk Densities
- Crushed stone / gravel: 1.5 t/yd³
- Pea gravel: 1.4 t/yd³
- River rock: 1.35 t/yd³
- Decomposed granite: 1.35 t/yd³
- Lava rock: 0.8 t/yd³

### Common Questions
Q: How much gravel for a 10×20 ft driveway at 4 inches deep?
A: 66.7 cu ft = 2.47 yd³ = ~3.7 tons of crushed stone. Add 10% for compaction.

Q: How deep should gravel be for a driveway?
A: 4–6 inch base of larger crushed stone (#3 or #4), topped with 2–3 inches of finish gravel. Total 6–8 inches for light vehicles.

Q: How much does a ton of gravel cover?
A: Pea gravel covers roughly 54 sq ft at 3 inches deep, or ~100 sq ft at 2 inches deep.

Q: What is the difference between cubic yards and tons?
A: Cubic yards measure volume; tons measure weight. Multiply yd³ by bulk density to convert.

---

## Mulch Calculator

URL: https://concrete.mrknowitall.net/mulch/

### Formulas
- vol_ft³ = length_ft × width_ft × depth_ft
- vol_yd³ = vol_ft³ ÷ 27
- 2 cu ft bags = ceil(vol_ft³ ÷ 2)  — standard bag at Lowe's / Home Depot
- 3 cu ft bags = ceil(vol_ft³ ÷ 3)

### Common Questions
Q: How much mulch for a 10×20 ft bed at 3 inches deep?
A: 50 cu ft = 1.85 yd³ = 25 bags of 2 cu ft mulch or 17 bags of 3 cu ft mulch.

Q: How deep should mulch be?
A: 2–4 inches. Standard recommendation is 3 inches — enough to suppress weeds and retain moisture without smothering roots. Keep mulch 2–3 inches away from plant stems and trunks.

Q: How many bags of mulch equal a cubic yard?
A: 27 cu ft per yd³ ÷ 2 cu ft per bag = 13.5 bags (2 cu ft); ÷ 3 cu ft = 9 bags (3 cu ft).

Q: When is bulk mulch more economical than bags?
A: Typically above 3 cubic yards. Bulk delivery is almost always cheaper per yard than bagged mulch.

Q: How often should mulch be replaced?
A: Top up every 1–2 years. Add a fresh 1–2 inch layer rather than replacing the entire bed.

---

## Topsoil Calculator

URL: https://concrete.mrknowitall.net/topsoil/

### Formulas
- vol_ft³ = length_ft × width_ft × depth_ft
- vol_yd³ = vol_ft³ ÷ 27
- Recommended order = vol_yd³ × 1.10 to 1.15 (10–15% settling allowance)

### Common Questions
Q: How much topsoil for a 10×20 ft area at 4 inches deep?
A: 66.7 cu ft = 2.47 yd³. With 10% settling, order 2.72 yd³.

Q: Why order extra for settling?
A: Bulk topsoil compacts 10–15% after watering and foot traffic. Order extra to end up at your target grade.

Q: How deep should topsoil be for a new lawn?
A: Minimum 4 inches; 6 inches preferred on poor or compacted native soil.

Q: How much does a cubic yard of topsoil weigh?
A: Roughly 1,800–2,200 lbs dry; over a ton when wet.

Q: Difference between topsoil and fill dirt?
A: Topsoil is nutrient-rich upper soil that supports plant growth. Fill dirt is subsoil used to fill voids — it contains little organic matter and should not be used as a growing medium.

---

## Sand Calculator

URL: https://concrete.mrknowitall.net/sand/

### Formulas
- vol_ft³ = length_ft × width_ft × depth_ft
- vol_yd³ = vol_ft³ ÷ 27
- tons = vol_yd³ × bulk_density

### Bulk Densities
- Dry sand: 1.35 t/yd³
- Wet / packed sand: 1.68 t/yd³
- Fill sand: 1.28 t/yd³
- Mason sand: 1.44 t/yd³
- Play sand: 1.20 t/yd³

### Common Questions
Q: How much sand for a 10×10 ft area at 2 inches deep?
A: 16.7 cu ft = 0.62 yd³ = ~0.84 tons of dry sand.

Q: How deep should the sand base be under pavers?
A: Exactly 1 inch of coarse bedding sand over a 4-inch compacted gravel sub-base.

Q: Difference between play sand and fill sand?
A: Play sand is washed and rounded — safe for sandboxes. Fill sand is coarser and used for sub-base. Never use fill sand in a sandbox.

Q: Can I use sand to level a lawn?
A: Pure sand is not recommended — use a 50/50 sand/compost topdressing mix instead.

---

## Sod Calculator

URL: https://concrete.mrknowitall.net/sod/

### Formulas
- area_ft² = length_ft × width_ft
- rolls = ceil(area_ft² × overage_multiplier ÷ 10)   — 1 roll = 10 sq ft
- pallets = ceil(area_ft² × overage_multiplier ÷ 450) — 1 pallet = 450 sq ft
- Recommended overage: 5% for straight rectangular areas; 10% for curved edges

### Common Questions
Q: How many rolls of sod for a 20×20 ft lawn?
A: 400 sq ft ÷ 10 sq ft per roll = 40 rolls + 5% = 42 rolls (less than one pallet).

Q: How much does a pallet of sod cover?
A: 450–504 sq ft depending on supplier. This calculator uses 450 sq ft as a conservative estimate.

Q: When is the best time to lay sod?
A: Cool-season grasses (fescue, bluegrass): fall or early spring. Warm-season grasses (Bermuda, zoysia, St. Augustine): late spring through summer when soil is above 60°F.

Q: How long does sod take to root?
A: 2–6 weeks. Keep it moist and off the lawn. It has rooted when you tug a corner and it resists.

Q: How do I prepare ground for sod?
A: Till 4–6 inches, remove debris, grade away from structures, add topsoil if needed, firm the surface, water lightly before laying.
